toolbar.garmin_title: Downloads the GPX and opens Garmin Connect to import it

upload.subtitle: Upload a route, add points and export your GPX
upload.desktop_hint: Recommended on desktop: the full experience works better on a larger screen.
upload.tutorial_title: See the workflow before you open the editor
upload.tutorial_intro: The flow is built for long rides: upload the GPX, mark key points, tune pacing zones, and leave with an export that is ready for Garmin or for print.
upload.tutorial_step1_title: Split the route and preview the effort
upload.tutorial_step1_body: Load a GPX and color each block with its target zone and watts. The map and elevation profile make it obvious where to push and where to save your legs.
upload.tutorial_step2_title: Add climbs, notes and feed zones
upload.tutorial_step2_body: Click directly on the route or add points by kilometer. Everything stays organized in the sidebar so the important moments of the day are easy to review.
upload.tutorial_step3_title: Create a clean image for race day or training
upload.tutorial_step3_body: Export the paced map, a horizontal print strip or a vertical cue sheet that you can stick on the bike and read at a glance.

map.no_route: Upload a GPX file to view the route
map.click_hint: Click on the route to add a point

sidebar.no_route: Upload a GPX file to get started
sidebar.click_hint: Click on the route to add points
